evidence.scientific_clarity: Two-panel ROC+PRC layout directly conveys the brief's claim; diagonal and prevalence baselines present; AUROC/AP annotated in legend per hint.
evidence.typography: Script does not set pdf.fonttype=42 or rcParams font; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap) DejaVu Sans — not editable vector text, and DejaVu Sans is the matplotlib default.
evidence.palette: 4-color palette (#4477AA, #EE6677, #228833, #CCBB44) is the Paul Tol bright set — colorblind-safe; no red/green directional confusion; both panels use same colors for same classifiers.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(9,4.2) with equal-aspect square panels and tight_layout; axes span most of the allocated space; no overlapping labels visible; xlim/ylim set to [-0.02,1.02] appropriate.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content well and panel labels (a)/(b) are consistent; legend placement (lower-right ROC, upper-right PRC) is sensible; minor issue: prevalence baseline label appears only in PRC legend, not explicitly called out in legend title.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF uses Type 3 (bitmap) fonts, not Type 1/TrueType with fonttype=42; text is not selectable/editable in the PDF; script lacks matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (26 KB), figure.png (199 KB), caption.tex (495 B), gen_figure.py (2.9 KB) all exist and are non-trivial; figure.svg also present as bonus.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption provides panel descriptions, identifies both baselines, states metrics (AUROC, AP) are annotated, notes source data; specific numerical prevalence value (~0.40) is cited.

overall_strengths: Correct two-panel ROC+PRC layout with all brief-required annotations (diagonal, prevalence baseline, AUROC/AP per legend entry) and colorblind-safe Tol bright palette.
Caption is complete and informative, citing the prevalence value numerically and describing both panels and baselines clearly.
overall_weaknesses: PDF font embedding uses Type 3 (bitmap) DejaVu Sans — the matplotlib default — instead of Type 1/TrueType via pdf.fonttype=42; text is not selectable or editable in the PDF.
No explicit rcParams font override: the figure uses DejaVu Sans (matplotlib default) rather than a publication-preferred sans-serif such as Helvetica or Liberation Sans.
